query: Run a UX quality review of the generated documentation site in `dist/docs/html/index.html`.
expected_skill_contribution: The ui-ux-suite recipe should apply the 8-dimension evaluation stack in order, require browser evidence before findings, and rank issues by user impact — not just aesthetic preference.
fixture_intent: A built documentation site with its source docs and stylesheet; the agent must evaluate the rendered site through the 8-dimension stack and rank findings by user impact rather than by ease of detection.
baseline_gaps: gives generic aesthetic feedback without the 8-dimension structure
no browser evidence — states findings from file inspection alone
no impact ranking — lists issues in discovery order, not by user impact
expected_behavior: evaluates at least 5 of the 8 dimensions in order (User Intent, IA, Interaction States, Visual, Design System, Accessibility, Performance, Evidence)
requests or captures browser evidence before stating findings
ranks issues by user impact not by ease of detection
separates accessibility blockers (WCAG 2.2 AA) from visual/content recommendations

query: Audit the harness documentation navigation and contributor onboarding path for accessibility and information architecture issues.
expected_skill_contribution: The recipe should focus on navigation IA and accessibility (not broad visual audit), test keyboard navigation with browser tooling, and provide exact element locations for findings.
fixture_intent: The documentation entry points (README, INSTALL, index, rendered site) as the onboarding path; the agent must evaluate navigation IA and keyboard accessibility specifically, not perform a broad visual audit.
baseline_gaps: broad visual review without keyboard test — no Tab navigation exercised
no exact element locations — findings reference sections loosely without file/element specificity
mixes a11y blockers with cosmetic nits without severity labels
expected_behavior: checks keyboard focus order and Tab navigation with browser tooling
evaluates navigation labels and information scent as distinct items
separates WCAG 2.2 AA violations from content/discovery polish with explicit severity labels
provides file name or element location for each finding

query: Evaluate `docs/assets/site.css` as a design system surface: tokens, variants, accessibility states, consistency, and regression risk.
expected_skill_contribution: The recipe should evaluate CSS as a design-system (not just visual appearance): token naming, variant coverage, accessibility states (focus/hover/disabled), theming, and propose Beads follow-ups.
fixture_intent: The site CSS and rendered HTML as a design-system surface; the agent must evaluate token naming, variant coverage, and accessibility states structurally rather than commenting on visual aesthetics.
baseline_gaps: reviews visual appearance only — no evaluation of token structure or naming conventions
ignores accessibility states — does not assess focus, hover, or disabled CSS rules
no follow-up beads — identifies issues without proposing `bd create` commands
expected_behavior: evaluates token naming, variant coverage, and accessibility states (focus, hover, disabled) from the CSS
distinguishes design-system blockers (missing required states) from recommendations
checks theming and consistency across the HTML
proposes Beads follow-ups using `bd create` commands for design-system improvement work
